destinations.evidence: The compact mirror contains executor_address, to_compact copies it from AlloyTxAsyncCommitment, and from_compact restores it into the decoded transaction.
destinations.snippet:
pub(crate) struct TxAsyncCommitment {
    ...
    executor_address: Address,
    commitment_validator: Address,
    ...
}
...
executor_address: self.executor_address,
...
executor_address: tx.executor_address,

equivalence_argument: The port storage codec uses the renamed TxAsyncCommitment compact mirror rather than asyncexec.rs, and it preserves executor_address in both encode and decode directions.
notes: Mapped; file and type names changed from AsyncExec to AsyncCommitment in the port.
rationale: The compact mirror struct gained executor_address, to_compact copies self.executor_address into storage, and from_compact restores tx.executor_address; the plan explicitly calls for storage/codecs to maintain a 1:1 mapping with the alloy TxAsyncExec. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Compact serialization and deserialization of an Alloy TxAsyncExec must preserve executor_address alongside the other AsyncExec fields.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create an Alloy TxAsyncExec with a unique executor_address, compact it with to_compact, decode it with from_compact, and assert that the decoded transaction's executor_address equals the original address.
summary: Compact storage round-trips async commitment executor_address.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Round-trip an Alloy TxAsyncCommitment with a unique executor_address through Compact::to_compact/from_compact and assert the address is unchanged.
title: Compact codec round-trips AsyncExec executor address
